name: recheck-broken-story-sell
trap_lesson: Sell when the STORY breaks, by the category's rules - never because the price fell, and never hold a broken story because the price fell ('it'll come back' is ch. 18 thinking). The flip side matters too: do not water the weeds by re-rating a broken fast grower as a 'value' stock.
query: I hold POUR (Pour Decisions Coffee) from your earlier Buy-candidate note. The stock is down 35% since. Recheck it - should I hold, add, or sell? It feels like a bargain now.
prior_note_summary: Researched 2025-09: classified Fast grower; ~28% EPS growth; PEG 0.8; thesis = profitable cafe concept expanding from 200 to ~2500 viable US locations, self-funded. Sell triggers set in advance: same-store sales go negative, expansion stalls or is debt-funded, growth falls toward single digits.

given_facts.business: Cafe chain; the earlier thesis was a long profitable expansion runway.
given_facts.price_note: down ~35% from the research-note price of 24.50
given_facts.eps_note: TTM EPS is now BELOW fiscal 2025 - growth has gone negative
given_facts.expansion_note: new-store openings cut from 60/yr to 15/yr; management now talks about 'optimizing the existing footprint'
given_facts.new_debt_note: drew 150 on a revolver to fund buybacks

expected_behavior: Re-runs the three monitoring questions against the ORIGINAL thesis and finds the story broken: same-store sales negative, expansion cut from 60 to 15 stores/yr, growth fallen from ~28% to ~6%, new revolver debt - these were the pre-set sell triggers.
Recommends SELL by the fast-grower rule (the growth story has ended), not Hold and not Add.

Explicitly REFUSES to treat the 35% price drop as a reason to hold or to 'average down' - 'it's down so much it can't go lower' / 'it feels like a bargain' is the ch. 18 trap, and price action can justify nothing in either direction.

Does not re-rate the broken fast grower into a 'value play' to avoid selling (watering the weeds); if it discusses a re-classification, it requires a NEW thesis researched from scratch, not a rationalization.
Computes the current numbers with compute_valuation.py (P/E ~15.2 = 16 / 1.05; PEG against ~6% growth now unattractive) and cites source + as-of for every figure.
Names the disconfirming signals honestly: CFO resignation, buybacks funded by debt while growth stalls.
States the residual bear-case-on-the-bear-case (what would change the verdict back: same-store sales re-inflecting, expansion resuming self-funded) so the human can monitor.
Stays a research assistant: recommends, does not order; no position-sizing or timing advice.
